text_draft:
Pink Bear looked at the broken spoon. Then at the toys scattered across the floor.
The wooden train. The doll. The crayons.
An idea bloomed.
"Susie," Pink Bear whispered. "We need help."
Susie gathered the toys. She lined them up on the windowsill like small, colorful soldiers. Pink Bear stood in front of them all.
"We're going outside," Pink Bear announced to the toys. "All of us. Together."
For the first time since the snow fell, Pink Bear felt something shift.
Not hope exactly. But determination.
They weren't alone in this.
